title: Patent Reimbursement Schemes in India 2026 - Kerala Startup Guide | BookMyTM

blocks.text: Patent Reimbursement Schemes 2026
The Complete Guide for Kerala Startups & MSMEs
Explore Funding Schemes

Every great business starts with an idea, but an idea is only truly yours when it is legally protected. For many founders, inventors, and small business owners in India—especially in Kerala's booming startup ecosystem—the high costs associated with drafting, filing, and prosecuting a patent can feel like an insurmountable roadblock.

However, protecting your Intellectual Property (IP) doesn't have to drain your working capital. Both the Central Government of India and the State Government of Kerala have aggressively updated their Patent Reimbursement Schemes for 2026. These initiatives are designed to give you your money back, covering both government statutory fees and the professional charges of your IP attorney.

As Kerala’s No.1 trusted partner for intellectual property, BookMyTM (headquartered in Kochi) doesn't just file your patent. We structure your entire filing and billing process to ensure you successfully claim every rupee you are entitled to under these government schemes. Here is your comprehensive 2026 guide to patent funding, eligibility, and how to get your money back.
Why Kerala is the Best Place to File a Patent in 2026

If your business is incorporated in Kerala, you have access to one of the most generous state-level IP support systems in the country. The Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM) Patent Support Scheme actively funds innovation to help local startups compete globally.
1. The KSUM Patent Support Scheme
This scheme is designed to alleviate the financial burden of the entire patent lifecycle—from initial filing to the final grant.
Financial Assistance Limit

items: Indian Patents: Up to ₹2 Lakh per awarded patent.
International Patents: Up to ₹10 Lakh on a single subject matter.

blocks.text: What Charges Are Covered?

The KSUM scheme covers both government filing charges and the authorized consultant/IP attorney fees. This means the professional fees you pay to BookMyTM are reimbursable. (Note: Yearly renewal charges and appellate actions are typically excluded).
Who is Eligible?

items: Tech startups registered with DPIIT & incorporated in Kerala.
Student innovators pursuing academics through their IEDC.

blocks.text: 2. The MSME Innovative Scheme
Even if you are not a DPIIT-recognized tech startup, your business can still claim heavy patent subsidies if you hold an MSME Udyam Registration.
2026 Reimbursement Limits
Other IP Covered
This scheme is incredibly comprehensive. It also covers:
The MSME scheme provides up to 100% reimbursement of actual costs incurred, including the government fees and the facilitation/drafting fees charged by your IP consultant.
3. Facilitating Start-Ups IP Protection (SIPP)
Initiated by DPIIT, the SIPP scheme takes a different approach: it directly reduces your upfront costs rather than asking you to wait months for a reimbursement.
80% Statutory Fee Rebate
DPIIT-recognized startups receive an automatic 80% rebate on official patent filing fees.
Zero Professional Fees (via Facilitators)
Under this specific route, the professional fees of government-appointed facilitators are paid directly by the government. The startup only pays the drastically reduced statutory fees.
The Hidden Trap: Why Startups Fail to Get Reimbursed
The government has allocated crores of rupees for these schemes, yet many startups fail to get their money. Why? Improper documentation and non-compliant invoicing.

Government portals (like KSUM and MSME) have strict audit requirements. If your IP consultant gives you a single, generic invoice that says your reimbursement claim will be instantly rejected. The government requires a granular breakdown showing exactly what portion was the statutory government fee and what portion was the professional drafting fee. Furthermore, the invoice must be GST-compliant, and proof of payment (bank statements) must match perfectly.

blocks.text: Frequently Asked Questions

items.q: How long does it take to get the patent reimbursement from the Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM)?
items.a: Once BookMyTM helps you submit the meticulously prepared invoice, receipt, and patent proof to the KSUM portal, processing typically takes between 15 to 30 working days for the funds to be credited directly to your startup's bank account.

items.q: Do I need to pay BookMyTM first, or does the government pay you directly?
items.a: For the KSUM and MSME reimbursement schemes, you must pay your IP consultant (BookMyTM) and the government fees first. You then submit the original paid invoices and receipts to the respective government portal to claim your 100% reimbursement.

items.q: Can I claim reimbursement if my patent gets rejected?
items.a: Generally, no. Most major reimbursement schemes (especially the larger payouts under KSUM) are tied to the grant or successful publication/prosecution stages of the patent. This is why having BookMyTM expertly draft your patent to ensure it gets granted is critical to getting your money back.

items.q: Is Trademark registration also reimbursable for MSMEs?
items.a: Yes! Under the MSME Innovative Scheme, you can claim a reimbursement of up to ₹10,000 for a Trademark registration. BookMyTM can process both your Trademark and Patent reimbursements simultaneously.

items.q: I am an individual inventor in Kerala, not a registered company. Can I get a reimbursement?
items.a: If you are an individual student innovator applying through a recognized IEDC (Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Centre) in your college, KSUM provides support. However, for maximum benefits under SIPP or MSME schemes, registering a Private Limited Company or obtaining Udyam MSME registration is highly recommended. BookMyTM can assist you with business incorporation first to unlock these benefits.

blocks.text: Ready to protect your invention and claim your government funding?
Protecting your groundbreaking ideas shouldn't be a financial gamble. The funds are waiting for you in 2026—you just need the right legal partner to unlock them. Don't let your IP remain vulnerable.
